cards.title: Chapter 1
cards.text: {{Expand}} your {{horizons}} next year with our award-{{winning}} {{overseas}} study {{programs}}. {{Supercharge}} your {{studies}} and gain new {{global}} {{perspectives}} by {{immersing}} {{yourself}} in a {{foreign}} {{language}} and {{culture}}, with {{courses}} {{offered}} by {{prestigious}} {{universities}} in over 100 {{countries}}. Study under some of the world’s top {{professors}} {{alongside}} {{students}} from {{around}} the globe.Not only can you make {{friends}} from all over the world, but you will open doors to {{incredible}} {{opportunities}} in the {{future}}.Our {{graduates}} have gone on to hold {{positions}} in {{several}} {{multinational}} {{corporations}} {{utilizing}} the {{highly}} {{desirable}} {{skills}} and {{experience}} they {{gained}} with us. The world is {{moving}} {{towards}} a {{global}} {{future}}, so why not get ahead of the trend and have an {{incredible}} {{adventure}} with {{Global}} Study Group?{{Global}} Study Group does not {{guarantee}} nor {{assist}} in {{securing}} {{employment}} after the {{completion}} of {{programs}}. {{Flights}}, {{accommodation}}, and other {{incidental}} {{expenses}} are not {{included}}.

award.tip: Auto translated by MyMemory｜A judgment, sentence, or final decision. Specifically: The decision of arbitrators in a case submitted.

cards.title: New Chapter
cards.text:
Good {{morning}},

First, I’d like to say a big “thank you” to {{everyone}} who {{worked}} {{overtime}} over the {{weekend}} to {{complete}} this year’s stock count. You guys {{finished}} in {{record}} time!

That being said, there is still a lot to do before the end of this month. We {{finally}} {{received}} the men’s {{winter}} coats from our {{supplier}} {{yesterday}}, and we need to get them out on the floor before the early {{holiday}} sale {{starts}} on {{December}} 1. {{Additionally}}, we will be {{erecting}} two {{temporary}} {{stands}} near the {{entrance}} to {{display}} last year’s {{leftover}} stock for sale.

I know you are all doing your best, and I won’t {{forget}} it when I {{calculate}} this year’s {{bonuses}}. Keep it up!

{{Thanks}} again,

C. Smith

{{General}} {{Manager}}, C&M {{Fashion}}

weekend.tip: Auto translated by MyMemory｜The break in the working week, usually two days including the traditional holy or sabbath day. Thus in western countries, Saturday and Sunday.

leftover.tip: Auto translated by MyMemory｜Something left behind; an excess or remainder.

calculate.tip: Auto translated by MyMemory｜To determine the value of something or the solution to something by a mathematical process.

bonuses.tip: Auto translated by MyMemory｜Something extra that is good; an added benefit.

over.tip: Auto translated by MyMemory｜A set of six legal balls bowled.

count.tip: Auto translated by MyMemory｜The act of counting or tallying a quantity.

overtime.tip: Auto translated by MyMemory｜Working time outside of one's regular hours.

hi {{everyone}} and {{welcome}} back to {{simple}}
{{spoken}}
{{English}} I'm Jack and I hope you're all

{{speaking}} {{spontaneously}} or on the spot
{{without}}
{{preparation}} I {{recently}} {{received}} a

down when you're {{giving}} a {{presentation}}
or have {{practiced}} {{sentences}} {{beforehand}}
you might feel {{pretty}} {{confident}} you know
what you're going to say so it's easy to

this is {{because}} you're {{trying}} to think
about the right words {{grammar}} and
{{sentence}} {{structure}} all at once and that
can be
{{overwhelming}} the {{result}} you {{hesitate}}
take long {{pauses}} or even {{freeze}} up it's

let's get into today's
topic how to speak {{English}}
{{spontaneously}} {{speaking}} {{spontaneously}}
means that you don't plan every {{single}}

{{example}} right now I'm {{speaking}}
{{spontaneously}} to you I haven't {{written}}
every {{sentence}} {{beforehand}} the words just
come to me as I talk when you can speak
{{spontaneously}} it means you're not
{{constantly}} {{pausing}} to think about what
to say next while short {{pauses}} are
{{completely}} okay and even {{natural}} long
{{pauses}} can break your flow and make you

what are your plans for the {{weekend}} if
you take too long to reply {{because}}
you're {{trying}} to find the {{perfect}} words
you might feel stuck or {{Tongue}} Tied
that's when {{people}} get {{nervous}} and it's

{{hesitation}} often leads to what we call

this can hurt your {{fluency}} and
{{confidence}} which is why it's so
{{important}} to work on {{speaking}} {{without}}
{{overthinking}} now I know this {{sounds}}
{{tricky}} but but don't worry you can train
{{yourself}} to speak more
{{spontaneously}} and today I've got six
{{practical}} tips that will help you do
just that these six {{Solutions}} are your
{{action}} plan to help you start {{speaking}}
{{English}} {{without}} {{hesitating}} or
{{overthinking}} the goal is to make your
words flow {{naturally}} {{without}} {{constantly}}
{{thinking}} about {{grammer}} rules or
{{translating}} {{thoughts}} from your {{native}}
{{language}} over time speak {{speaking}} will
{{become}} more {{natural}} and words will come
to you more
{{easily}} so let's dive into those six
{{Solutions}} first you need to {{immerse}}
{{yourself}} in {{English}} the more you hear it
the more {{comfortable}} you'll get {{second}}
{{practice}} {{speaking}} out loud even if it's
just to {{yourself}} third think in {{English}}
{{instead}} of your {{native}} {{language}} {{fourth}}
use {{simple}} {{sentences}} at first and
{{gradually}} build up
{{complexity}} fifth {{engage}} in {{regular}}
{{conversation}} with {{others}} even if it's
just small talk {{finally}} don't be {{afraid}}
to make {{mistakes}} that's how you learn
{{remember}} it's all about {{practicing}} and
{{building}} your {{confidence}} step by step
with time you'll find {{yourself}} {{speaking}}
{{English}} more {{naturally}} {{without}} {{planning}}
every word let's get {{started}} with the
six {{solution}}
let's dive into {{solution}} one for
{{speaking}} {{English}} more {{fluently}} and
{{spontaneously}} the topic {{bringing}} {{Clarity}}
to your
{{thoughts}} as I {{mentioned}} before {{speaking}}
{{English}} {{spontaneously}} is tough if your
{{thoughts}} are {{unclear}} so let's break it
down when we speak what we say comes
{{directly}} from what we think our {{thoughts}}
are the {{foundation}} of our {{speech}} and if
your mind is clear about what you want
to {{express}} that {{confusion}} will show up
in your {{speech}} Let's {{imagine}} you're
about to speak but your {{thoughts}} are a
bit foggy maybe you're {{uncertain}} about
which word to use or you're not sure if
your {{grammar}} is right what {{happens}} next
you
{{hesitate}} that {{little}} pause might not

{{fluency}} and
{{spontaneity}} it's like being stuck in tra
{{traffic}} you know where you want to go
but you're {{unsure}} how to get there
{{because}} of the {{blocks}} in your mind
{{instead}} of {{focusing}} on the {{message}} you
want to share you end up {{worrying}} about
The Words which slows you down to be
able to speak {{clearly}} and {{naturally}} you
need to bring {{Clarity}} to your {{thoughts}}
but how can you do this {{especially}} when
{{thoughts}} and {{speech}} {{happen}} so {{quickly}}
often in
{{milliseconds}} let's think about this in
your {{native}} {{language}} you don't have this
{{problem}} why {{because}} you've {{practiced}} it
for so long that you don't need to
{{consciously}} think about the words if
{{someone}} asks how are you in your {{native}}
{{language}} you {{respond}} {{immediately}} {{without}}
{{hesitation}} but in {{English}} you might
pause {{because}} you're {{searching}} for the

we want to
{{reduce}} now let me share a {{technique}} that
can help you train your mind to think
{{clearly}} and speak more {{naturally}} in
{{English}} it's {{called}}
{{subvocalization}} this might sound
{{complicated}} but it's {{actually}} quite
{{simple}} {{subvocalization}} is when you
{{silently}} {{repeat}} words to {{yourself}} as you
read or think it's like {{hearing}} {{yourself}}
speak in your mind but {{without}} {{saying}}
the words out loud here's how it works
when you're {{reading}} {{something}} in {{English}}
{{whether}} it's a book an {{article}} or even
{{subtitles}} on a video you're not just
{{reading}} {{silently}} you're also {{mentally}}
{{pronouncing}} the words in your head this
{{internal}} Voice or sub {{vocalization}}
{{allows}} you to {{practice}} {{English}} {{structure}}
and {{Rhythm}} {{without}} {{needing}} to say the
words out loud over time this helps
train your brain to think in {{English}}
more {{clearly}} just as you do in your
{{native}} {{language}} let's take an {{example}}
{{imagine}} you're {{reading}} the {{sentence}} she
was {{standing}} on the {{balcony}} {{staring}} at
the
stars even {{though}} you're {{reading}}
{{silently}} you might {{notice}} that in your
mind you can hear the {{sentence}} with the
{{correct}} tone and {{Rhythm}} as if you were
{{saying}} it out loud this is
{{subvocalization}} at work it's like you're
{{practicing}} {{speaking}} in your head
{{preparing}} your brain for real
{{conversations}} {{without}} the {{pressure}} of
{{speaking}}
aloud by {{practicing}} this {{technique}}
you're not just {{reading}} for
{{understanding}} you're {{training}} your
{{subconscious}} mind to {{process}} {{English}}
{{faster}} and more {{clearly}} this way when it
comes time to speak you'll find the
right words and {{sentences}} {{coming}} to you
much more
{{naturally}} you've {{already}} {{prepared}} your
brain by {{feeding}} it with {{correct}}
{{structures}} and {{phrases}} {{during}} {{reading}}
{{another}} great {{benefit}} of {{subvocalization}}
is that it {{boosts}} your
{{confidence}} think about it the more you
{{practice}} {{thinking}} in {{English}} the more
{{comfortable}} you'll be when {{speaking}} it
you won't have to pause as much {{because}}
your brain {{already}} has a clear set of
words and {{phrases}} ready to go it's like
{{training}} for a race the more you
{{practice}} the {{better}} {{prepared}} you'll be
when it's time to run so the key to
{{improving}} {{spontaneity}} in {{English}} isn't
just about {{speaking}} more often it's
about {{thinking}} more {{clearly}} and {{training}}
your brain to be ready for those
{{unplanned}}
{{conversations}} when your {{thoughts}} are
clear your {{speech}} will {{follow}} in {{summary}}
{{bringing}} {{Clarity}} to your
{{thoughts}} is {{crucial}} for {{speaking}} {{English}}
{{naturally}} and {{confidently}} and
{{subvocalization}} is a {{powerful}} {{technique}}
that can help you {{achieve}} this
{{Clarity}} {{remember}} it's like {{rehearsing}} in
your mind before the real {{conversation}}
{{begins}} with {{regular}} {{practice}} your
{{subconscious}} mind will start to pick up
the {{patterns}} {{structures}} and {{vocabulary}}
you need and you'll be able to speak
more {{fluently}} {{without}} {{hesitating}} or
{{overthinking}} so start {{practicing}} this
today when you read don't just focus on
{{understanding}} the text hear the words in
your mind and let them flow {{naturally}}
over time this will make a huge
{{difference}} in your {{ability}} to speak
{{English}} with {{confidence}} and
{{spontaneity}} now let's dive into {{solution}}
two which is about {{training}} your
{{subconscious}} mind this is the most
{{important}} step in {{learning}} to speak
{{English}} {{fluently}} {{without}} {{overthinking}}
you see your {{subconscious}} mind plays a
huge role in how you {{process}} and use
{{language}} right now your {{subconscious}} is
fully {{comfortable}} with your {{native}}
{{language}} but for {{English}} to feel as
{{natural}} we need to train it when your
{{subconscious}} mind is {{familiar}} with
{{English}} you'll be able to speak {{without}}
{{feeling}} {{nervous}} or {{unsure}} for {{example}}
while you're {{listening}} to me right now
your {{subconscious}} is {{already}} {{absorbing}}
{{everything}} I say but here's the thing if
you don't keep {{revisiting}} or {{practicing}}
this {{information}} it will fade over time
the key is to keep {{practicing}} until
you're {{subconscious}} holds on to {{English}}
in the same way it does with your {{native}}
{{language}} so how do we train the
{{subconscious}} mind one of the best ways
is {{through}} {{listening}} {{listening}} to
{{English}} {{regularly}} {{creates}} an inner
{{environment}} where {{English}} feels {{familiar}}
and {{comfortable}} think of it like
{{training}} your brain {{muscles}} you're
{{creating}} a {{natural}} flow in your mind
where {{English}} {{becomes}} {{second}} {{nature}}
start by {{listening}} to {{content}} that's
{{reliable}} and {{fluent}} it could be {{anything}}
{{educational}} {{videos}} {{entertainment}}
{{lifestyle}} tips or even {{health}} {{related}}
{{topics}} {{YouTube}} is a {{treasure}} Trove of
{{English}} {{content}} you could also watch

{{podcasts}} the key is to make sure you're
{{hearing}} {{English}} that's {{spoken}} {{correctly}}
and {{Naturally}} by {{surrounding}} {{yourself}}
with good {{English}} {{content}} your
{{subconscious}} will start {{storing}} the
{{language}} {{anguage}} over time you'll {{notice}}
a big {{difference}} in how {{naturally}}
{{English}} comes to you when you
speak let's move on to {{solution}}
three which is all about {{learning}} to
think in
{{English}} now that we've {{talked}} about
{{Clarity}} of {{thought}} and using the
{{subconscious}} mind it's {{important}} to
focus on how we think if you want to
speak {{English}} {{fluently}} and
{{spontaneously}} you need to stop
{{translating}} from your {{native}} {{language}}
{{thinking}} in {{English}} is key here's what
{{happens}} when you're used to {{thinking}} in
your {{native}} {{language}} your brain works in
two steps first you think of {{something}}
in your {{native}} {{language}} then you try to
{{translate}} it into {{English}} this slows you
down and makes {{speaking}} more {{difficult}}
to break this habit start {{describing}}
{{things}} {{directly}} in {{English}} for {{example}}
if you see a book don't let your first
{{thought}} be in your {{native}} {{language}}
{{instead}} train your mind to {{immediately}}
think book in {{English}} the more you
{{practice}} the more {{natural}} it will {{become}}
over time this will help you {{respond}}
{{faster}} and with more {{confidence}} {{during}}
{{conversations}} the goal is to make
{{English}} your {{default}} {{thinking}} {{language}}
{{especially}} when you're {{speaking}} this
doesn't need to feel {{forced}} {{gradually}} it
will {{become}} a habit it the more you
{{immerse}} {{yourself}} in {{English}} the {{easier}}
it {{becomes}} to think and speak {{naturally}}
{{without}} {{translating}} {{remember}} the key to
{{speaking}}
{{spontaneously}} is {{thinking}} in {{English}} as
you {{practice}} this daily you'll see
{{yourself}} {{becoming}} more {{fluent}} and
{{confident}} keep at it and you'll {{notice}}
the
{{difference}} let's dive {{deeper}} into our
{{discussion}} with war {{solution}} 4 which is
all about {{focusing}} on Flow not
flaws when {{learning}} to speak {{English}}
many {{people}} get stuck {{because}} they focus
too much on the {{mistakes}} they make or
what we call flaws but here's the key
focus on Flow not on
flaws what do we mean by
flow flow {{refers}} to the {{smoothness}} of
your {{speech}} how {{naturally}} the words come
out and how {{easily}} the {{conversation}}
moves {{forward}} {{fluency}} is after all about
{{having}} a good flow too often {{English}}
{{Learners}} are {{afraid}} to make {{mistakes}}
they stop mids {{sentence}} {{overthink}} every
word or {{hesitate}} before {{speaking}} {{because}}
they don't want to say {{something}} wrong
but the truth is {{mistakes}} are a {{natural}}
part of
{{learning}} they're a sign that you're
{{trying}} that you're {{pushing}} {{yourself}} to
{{improve}} so {{instead}} of {{focusing}} on these
{{mistakes}} shift your {{attention}} to
{{maintaining}} a {{steady}} flow when you speak
think of {{speaking}} {{English}} like a
{{performance}} on stage when an actor
{{performs}} the {{audience}} sees the {{result}}
not the {{rehearsals}} the actor might have
made {{mistakes}} in {{practice}} but {{during}} the
{{performance}} the focus is on {{delivering}}
the lines with {{confidence}} the same goes
for you when you're in a {{conversation}}
it's time to form {{whether}} you make small
{{mistakes}} in {{grammar}} or {{pronunciation}}
doesn't {{matter}} as much as the {{overall}}
flow of your {{speech}} the goal is to keep
going the more you {{practice}} the more
{{naturally}} the words will come flow leads
to {{fluency}} and {{fluency}} leads to
{{spontaneity}} as you keep {{speaking}} {{without}}
{{getting}} stuck on your {{mistakes}} your mind
will {{become}} more {{accustomed}} to {{forming}}
{{sentences}} {{smoothly}}
over time you'll find that you're able
to speak {{English}} more {{confidently}} and
with fewer
{{errors}} now let's talk about {{solution}}
five which {{focuses}} on using small
{{sentence}}
{{structures}} when {{learning}} to speak
{{English}} using {{smaller}} {{sentence}}
{{structures}} can be {{extremely}} {{helpful}} many
{{Learners}} {{struggle}} with long {{complex}}
{{sentences}} which often {{become}} {{jumbled}} and
hard to {{manage}} {{AG}} when {{translating}} from
your {{native}} {{language}} you might try to
cram too much {{information}} into one
{{sentence}} this can cause {{confusion}} and
lead to {{mistakes}} {{instead}} of {{trying}} to
{{create}} long {{sentences}} focus on
{{expressing}} one idea at a time short
clear {{sentences}} are {{easier}} to {{handle}} and
will help you speak more {{fluently}} and
{{spontaneously}} for {{example}} {{instead}} of
{{saying}} I want to go to the store {{because}}
I need to buy some {{groceries}} but I also
need to pick up my {{laundry}} you can break
it down into two {{sentences}} I need to go
to the store to buy some {{groceries}} after
that I'll pick up my
{{laundry}} using short {{sentences}} {{allows}} you
to think {{clearly}} and stay {{focused}} on one
{{thought}} at a time this not only makes
your {{speech}} more {{fluent}} but also helps
you avoid {{mistakes}} in {{grammar}} and
{{sentence}} {{structure}} if you're {{aiming}} for
{{accuracy}} in your {{speech}}
small {{sentences}} are a great way to
{{achieve}} that it's {{important}} to {{remember}}
that short {{sentences}} don't mean
{{incomplete}}
{{sentences}} you {{should}} still aim to use
{{complete}} {{thoughts}} but avoid {{cramming}} too
many ideas into one {{sentence}} this
{{approach}} will make your {{speech}} {{clearer}}
{{easier}} to {{follow}} and less {{likely}} to get
mixed up {{additionally}} {{shorter}} {{sentences}}
give you the {{opportunity}} to pause take a
{{breath}} and {{prepare}} for the next
{{thought}} this helps {{maintain}} a {{steady}}
flow of {{speech}} {{without}} {{overloading}} your
brain with too much {{information}} at once
over time as you {{become}} more {{comfortable}}
you can {{gradually}} {{increase}} the {{length}} of
your {{sentences}} but at the {{beginning}}
focus on {{keeping}} {{things}} {{simple}} and clear
our sixth {{solution}} is {{perhaps}} the most
{{important}} {{practice}} {{regularly}} in
{{situations}} no {{matter}} how much you study
{{grammar}} or
{{vocabulary}} the real {{progress}} {{happens}}
when you use {{English}} in real life
{{situations}} you need to put {{yourself}} in
{{environments}} where {{speaking}} {{English}}
comes {{naturally}} this could be in
{{conversations}} with {{friends}} {{joining}}
{{language}} {{groups}} or even {{chatting}} with

{{comfortable}}
{{conversations}} and {{gradually}} move on to

speak {{English}} more {{regularly}} your mind
will start to adapt you'll begin to
think less about {{grammar}} or {{sentence}}
{{structure}} and your {{speech}} will {{become}}
more
{{automatic}} {{speaking}} {{English}} {{regularly}}
{{builds}} {{fluency}} just like {{muscle}}
{{memory}} the more you use {{English}} in real
life {{situations}} the more {{familiar}} your
brain {{becomes}} with {{forming}} {{sentences}} on
the spot
it's like {{learning}} to ride a bike the
more you do it the more {{natural}} it
{{becomes}} and don't worry about {{making}}

{{communicating}} your goal is to get your

{{perfect}} {{communication}} is more {{important}}
than
{{Perfection}} if you can get your point
{{across}} that's a {{success}} over time your
{{fluency}} and {{spontaneity}} will {{improve}} and
you'll make fewer {{mistakes}} but don't let

{{speaking}} to {{summarize}} here are the six

{{subconscious}}
mind {{immerse}} {{yourself}} in {{English}} by

adapt two
bring {{Clarity}} to your
{{thoughts}} {{subvocalization}} and {{thinking}} in
{{English}} will help you {{organize}} your
{{thoughts}} more {{clearly}} {{making}} it {{easier}}
to speak
{{spontaneously}} three think in
{{English}} avoid the {{translation}} {{process}}
train {{yourself}} to think {{directly}} in
{{English}} to {{reduce}} {{hesitation}} and {{improve}}
{{spontaneity}} four focus on Flow not on
flaws {{maintain}} a {{steady}} flow of {{speech}}
even if you make {{mistakes}} {{fluency}} comes
from {{continuous}} {{smooth}} {{speaking}} not from
{{perfect}} {{grammar}} five use small {{sentence}}
{{structures}} break down your {{thoughts}} into
clear {{concise}} {{sentences}} this will make
it {{easier}} to stay {{focused}} and {{reduce}}
{{mistakes}} six {{practice}} {{regularly}} in real
{{situations}} put {{yourself}} in
{{English}}-{{speaking}} {{environment}} ments as
often as {{possible}} real life {{practice}} is
the best way to {{improve}} {{fluency}} and
{{spontaneity}} now let's talk about a few
extra tips that will help you on your
{{journey}} to
{{fluency}} set small {{achievable}}
goals it's easy to feel {{overwhelmed}} when
{{learning}} a new {{language}} so break your
{{learning}} down into {{smaller}} tasks for
{{example}} set a goal to have a 5-{{minute}}

write a short {{paragraph}} about your day
over time {{increase}} the {{difficulty}} of
these tasks this will help you track
your {{progress}} and build your {{confidence}}
as you {{achieve}} each
goal {{consistency}} is
key even if you only have time for a
short {{practice}} {{session}} each day {{regular}}
{{practice}} is more {{effective}} than {{cramming}}
once a week daily {{exposure}} to {{English}}
will keep your brain {{engaged}} with the
{{language}} and help you {{recall}} {{vocabulary}}
and {{sentence}} {{structures}} when
{{needed}} {{celebrate}} your
{{progress}} {{learning}} a {{language}} is a long
{{journey}} and it's {{important}} to {{recognize}}
your {{achievements}} along the way {{whether}}
you've held your first {{conversation}}
{{without}} {{hesitation}} or {{understood}} a
{{complex}} {{sentence}} in {{English}} {{celebrate}}
these
{{Milestones}} this {{positive}} {{reinforcement}}
will keep you {{motivated}} to {{continue}}

{{gradually}} as you get more {{comfortable}}
with {{English}} push your {{limits}} a {{little}}
try {{discussing}} more {{complex}} {{topics}} or
{{practice}} {{talking}} about {{abstract}} {{Concepts}}
like {{happiness}} or {{culture}} this will
{{expand}} your {{vocabulary}} and {{improve}} your
{{ability}} to think and {{respond}} in {{English}}
on the
spot {{finally}} stay {{patient}} and
{{positive}} {{language}} {{learning}} can be
{{frustrating}} at times but {{mistakes}} are a
{{natural}} part of the {{process}} keep a
{{positive}} {{attitude}} and {{remember}} that
every {{mistake}} {{brings}} you {{closer}} to

bulleted.geminiEx: Please provide the summary in a bulleted list.
bulleted.geminiTwEx: 請用列點清單的方式提供摘要。

geminiRaw.ex: Please provide the summary in a bulleted list.

bullet.tip: A projectile, usually of metal, shot from a gun at high speed.

definitions.def: A projectile, usually of metal, shot from a gun at high speed.
An entire round of unfired ammunition for a firearm, including the projectile, the cartridge casing, the propellant charge, etc.
Ammunition for a sling or slingshot which has been manufactured for such use.
To draw attention to (text) by, or as if by, placing a graphic bullet in front of it.
To speed, like a bullet.
definitions.example: Their debut started slow, but bulleted to number six in its fourth week.

definitions.def: To make a shot, especially with great speed.
definitions.example: He bulleted a header for his first score of the season.

approve.tip: To sanction officially; to ratify; to confirm; to set as satisfactory.

validat.geminiEx: The data was validated by the research team to ensure its accuracy.
validat.geminiTwEx: 研究團隊驗證了這些數據以確保其準確性。

geminiRaw.ex: The data was validated by the research team to ensure its accuracy.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 研究團隊驗證了這些數據以確保其準確性。

check.tip: A situation in which the king is directly threatened by an opposing piece.

person.tip: An individual; usually a human being.

definitions.def: Major; in the major mode.
definitions.example: C dur

during.geminiEx: We stayed in a hotel during our vacation.
during.geminiTwEx: 我們在度假期間住在飯店裡。

geminiRaw.ex: We stayed in a hotel during our vacation.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 我們在度假期間住在飯店裡。

following.tip: To go after; to pursue; to move behind in the same path or direction.

geminiRaw.ex: Please answer the following questions.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 請回答下列問題。

spontaneously.tip: In a spontaneous manner; naturally; voluntarily.

definitions.def: In a spontaneous manner; naturally; voluntarily.

spontaneously.geminiEx: The crowd spontaneously started singing when the music began.
spontaneously.geminiTwEx: 當音樂響起時，人群自發地開始歌唱。

geminiRaw.ex: The crowd spontaneously started singing when the music began.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 當音樂響起時，人群自發地開始歌唱。

welcome.tip: The act of greeting someone’s arrival, especially by saying "Welcome!"; reception.
welcome.geminiEx: You are welcome to join us for dinner tonight.
welcome.geminiTwEx: 歡迎你今晚加入我們的晚餐。

geminiRaw.ex: You are welcome to join us for dinner tonight.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 歡迎你今晚加入我們的晚餐。

definitions: [noun] The act of greeting someone’s arrival, especially by saying "Welcome!"; reception.
[noun] The utterance of such a greeting.
[verb] To affirm or greet the arrival of someone, especially by saying "Welcome!".
[verb] To accept something willingly or gladly.
[adjective] Whose arrival is a cause of joy; received with gladness; admitted willingly to the house, entertainment, or company.
[adjective] Producing gladness.
[interjection] Greeting given upon someone's arrival.
[interjection] Ellipsis of you're welcome.

totally.tip: To the fullest extent or degree.

definitions: [noun] A cutting; a part cut out from the rest of something.
[noun] A part, piece, subdivision of anything.
[verb] To cut, divide or separate into pieces.
[verb] To reduce to the degree of thinness required for study with the microscope.

overtime.tip: Working time outside of one's regular hours.
overtime.geminiEx: He has been working a lot of overtime lately to finish the project.
overtime.geminiTwEx: 他最近為了完成專案一直在大量加班。

geminiRaw.ex: He has been working a lot of overtime lately to finish the project.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 他最近為了完成專案一直在大量加班。

definitions.def: Working time outside of one's regular hours.
definitions.example: Workers are usually paid extra for working overtime.

definitions.def: The rate of pay, usually higher, for work done outside of or in addition to regular hours.
An extra period of play when a contest has a tie score at the end of regulation. (British: extra time.)
definitions.example: That last-second shot ties the game 99-99 and sends it to overtime!

definitions.def: To measure something incorrectly, as taking more time than it actually did.
Exceeding regular working hours.
Beyond the normal or usual extent.

over.tip: A set of six legal balls bowled.

weekend.tip: The break in the working week, usually two days including the traditional holy or sabbath day. Thus in western countries, Saturday and Sunday.

speak.tip: A low class bar, a speakeasy.
speak.geminiEx: She can speak four languages fluently.
speak.geminiTwEx: 她能流利地說四種語言。

geminiRaw.ex: She can speak four languages fluently.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 她能流利地說四種語言。

definitions.def: A low class bar, a speakeasy.
Language, jargon, or terminology used uniquely in a particular environment or group.
definitions.example: Corporate speak; IT speak.

definitions.def: Speech, conversation.
To communicate with one's voice, to say words out loud.
definitions.example: I was so surprised I couldn't speak.

definitions.def: To have a conversation.
definitions.example: It's been ages since we've spoken.

definitions.def: (by extension) To communicate or converse by some means other than orally, such as writing or facial expressions.
definitions.example: Actions speak louder than words.

count.tip: The act of counting or tallying a quantity.
count.geminiEx: Can you count how many people are in the room?
count.geminiTwEx: 你能數數看房間裡有多少人嗎？

geminiRaw.ex: Can you count how many people are in the room?
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 你能數數看房間裡有多少人嗎？

definitions.def: The act of counting or tallying a quantity.
definitions.example: Give the chairs a quick count to check if we have enough.

definitions.def: The result of a tally that reveals the number of items in a set; a quantity counted.
A countdown.
To recite numbers in sequence.
definitions.example: Can you count to a hundred?

definitions.def: To determine the number (of objects in a group).
definitions.example: There are three apples; count them.

definitions.def: To be of significance; to matter.
definitions.example: Your views don’t count here.    It does count if you cheat with someone when you’re drunk.

definitions.def: (grammar) Countable.

bonus.tip: Something extra that is good; an added benefit.
bonus.geminiEx: All employees received a year-end bonus for their hard work.
bonus.geminiTwEx: 所有員工都因為辛勤工作而領到了年終獎金。

geminiRaw.ex: All employees received a year-end bonus for their hard work.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 所有員工都因為辛勤工作而領到了年終獎金。

calculate.tip: To determine the value of something or the solution to something by a mathematical process.

leftover.tip: Something left behind; an excess or remainder.
leftover.geminiEx: We ate the leftovers for lunch the next day.
leftover.geminiTwEx: 我們隔天午餐吃了剩下的菜。

geminiRaw.ex: We ate the leftovers for lunch the next day.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 我們隔天午餐吃了剩下的菜。

definitions.def: Something left behind; an excess or remainder.
definitions.example: It's a leftover from yesterday, but it's still perfectly good.

definitions.def: (chiefly in the plural, usually of food) Remaining after a meal is complete or eaten for a later meal or snack.
definitions.example: Not leftovers again!

definitions.def: Remaining; left behind; extra; in reserve.
definitions.example: Do you want some of the leftover supplies from the event?

spot.tip: A round or irregular patch on the surface of a thing having a different color, texture etc. and generally round in shape.
spot.geminiTw: 地點、斑點 / 發現、認出 / 現貨的
spot.geminiEx: I found a perfect spot for a picnic in the park.
spot.geminiTwEx: 我在公園裡找到了一個野餐的絕佳地點。

geminiRaw.tw: 地點、斑點 / 發現、認出 / 現貨的
geminiRaw.ex: I found a perfect spot for a picnic in the park.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 我在公園裡找到了一個野餐的絕佳地點。

definitions.def: A round or irregular patch on the surface of a thing having a different color, texture etc. and generally round in shape.
definitions.example: The leopard is noted for the spots of color in its fur.

definitions.def: A stain or disfiguring mark.
definitions.example: I have tried everything, and I can’t get this spot out.

definitions.def: A pimple, papule or pustule.
definitions.example: I think she's got chicken pox; she's covered in spots.

definitions.def: To see, find; to pick out, notice, locate, distinguish or identify.
definitions.example: Try to spot the differences between these two pictures.

definitions.def: To loan a small amount of money to someone.
definitions.example: I’ll spot you ten dollars for lunch.

definitions.def: To stain; to leave a spot (on).
definitions.example: Hard water will spot if it is left on a surface.

definitions.def: Available on the spot; for immediate payment or delivery.
definitions.example: spot wheat; spot cash; a spot contract

beforehand.tip: In comfortable circumstances as regards property; forehanded.
beforehand.geminiEx: If you make a reservation beforehand, you won't have to wait in line.
beforehand.geminiTwEx: 如果你事先預約，就不用排隊了。

geminiRaw.ex: If you make a reservation beforehand, you won't have to wait in line.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 如果你事先預約，就不用排隊了。

definitions: [adjective] In comfortable circumstances as regards property; forehanded.
[adjective] (often followed by with) In a state of anticipation or preoccupation.
[adverb] At an earlier or preceding time.

learn.geminiEx: She wants to learn how to play the piano.

geminiRaw.ex: She wants to learn how to play the piano.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 她想學習如何彈鋼琴。

everyone.geminiEx: Everyone is invited to the party.
everyone.geminiTwEx: 每個人都受邀參加派對。

geminiRaw.tw_ex: 每個人都受邀參加派對。

definitions.def: Every person.

preparation.tip: The act of preparing or getting ready.
preparation.geminiEx: The team made careful preparation for the upcoming project.
preparation.geminiTwEx: 團隊為即將到來的專案做了細心的準備。

geminiRaw.ex: The team made careful preparation for the upcoming project.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 團隊為即將到來的專案做了細心的準備。

definitions.def: The act of preparing or getting ready.
The state of being prepared; readiness.
That which is prepared.
The visible horizontal line or point (in all directions) that appears to connect the Earth to the sky.
definitions.example: A tall building was visible on the horizon.

definitions.def: The range or limit of one's knowledge, experience or interest; a boundary or threshold.
definitions.example: Some students take a gap year after finishing high school to broaden their horizons.

definitions.def: The range or limit of any dimension in which one exists.

horizon.geminiEx: The sun began to sink below the horizon.
horizon.geminiTwEx: 太陽開始沒入地平線之下。

geminiRaw.ex: The sun began to sink below the horizon.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 太陽開始沒入地平線之下。

award.tip: A judgment, sentence, or final decision. Specifically: The decision of arbitrators in a case submitted.
award.geminiEx: The committee decided to award her the first prize for her outstanding performance.
award.geminiTwEx: 委員會決定因她傑出的表現而頒發第一名獎項給她。

geminiRaw.ex: The committee decided to award her the first prize for her outstanding performance.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 委員會決定因她傑出的表現而頒發第一名獎項給她。

supercharge.tip: A charge borne upon an ordinary or other charge.
supercharge.geminiTw: 增強；使充滿活力；給（引擎）加裝增壓器
supercharge.geminiEx: The new marketing strategy will supercharge our sales growth this year.
supercharge.geminiTwEx: 新的行銷策略將會大幅提升我們今年的銷售成長。

geminiRaw.tw: 增強；使充滿活力；給（引擎）加裝增壓器

geminiRaw.tw_ex: 新的行銷策略將會大幅提升我們今年的銷售成長。

definitions: [noun] A charge borne upon an ordinary or other charge.
[verb] To increase the power of an internal combustion engine (either Otto or Diesel cycle) by compressing the inlet air with power extracted from the crankshaft.
[verb] To make faster or more powerful.
[noun] The act of gaining; acquisition.
[noun] What is gained.
[verb] To acquire possession of.
[verb] To have or receive advantage or profit; to acquire gain; to grow rich; to advance in interest, health, or happiness; to make progress.
gain.geminiEx: He worked hard to gain experience in the field.
gain.geminiTwEx: 他努力工作以在該領域獲得經驗。

geminiRaw.ex: He worked hard to gain experience in the field.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 他努力工作以在該領域獲得經驗。

next.tip: The one that follows after this one.

geminiRaw.ex: Who is next in line?
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 下一個排隊的人是誰？

definitions.def: The one that follows after this one.
definitions.example: Next, please, don't hold up the queue!

definitions.def: Nearest in place or position, having nothing similar intervening; adjoining.
Nearest in order, succession, or rank; immediately following (or sometimes preceding) in order.
definitions.example: the next chapter; the next man I see; the next week; the Sunday next before Easter

definitions.def: Following in a hypothetical sequence of some kind.
definitions.example: The man was driven by his love for money and his desire to become the next Bill Gates.

definitions.def: In a time, place, rank or sequence closest or following.
definitions.example: It's the next best thing to ice cream.

definitions.def: On the first subsequent occasion.
definitions.example: Financial panic, earthquakes, oil spills, riots. What comes next?

definitions.def: On the side of; nearest or adjacent to; next to.

include.tip: A piece of source code or other content that is dynamically retrieved for inclusion in another item.
include.geminiEx: The price includes breakfast and dinner.

geminiRaw.ex: The price includes breakfast and dinner.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 價格包含早餐和晚餐。

definitions: [noun] A piece of source code or other content that is dynamically retrieved for inclusion in another item.
[verb] To bring into a group, class, set, or total as a (new) part or member.
[verb] To contain, as parts of a whole; to comprehend.
[noun] A set of structured activities.
[noun] A leaflet listing information about a play, game or other activity.
[verb] To enter a program or other instructions into (a computer or other electronic device) to instruct it to do a particular task.
[verb] To develop (software) by writing program code.

definitions.def: A globally scoped identifier.
Concerning all parts of the world.
definitions.example: Pollution is a global problem.

definitions.def: Pertaining to the whole of something; total, universal:
Spherical, ball-shaped.
definitions.example: In the center was a small, global mass.

definitions.def: In the global manner; world-wide.

global.geminiEx: Climate change is a global issue that requires international cooperation.
global.geminiTwEx: 氣候變遷是一個需要國際合作的全球性議題。

geminiRaw.ex: Climate change is a global issue that requires international cooperation.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 氣候變遷是一個需要國際合作的全球性議題。

geminiRaw.ex: You should believe in yourself.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 你應該相信你自己。

definitions.def: (reflexive pronoun) Your own self (singular).
definitions.example: Be careful with that fire or you'll burn yourself.

definitions.def: You (singular); used emphatically, especially to indicate exclusiveness of the referent's participation in the predicate, i.e., that no one else is involved.
definitions.example: After a good night's sleep you'll feel like yourself again.

definitions: [verb] To put under the surface of a liquid; to dunk.
[verb] To involve or engage deeply.
[adjective] Immersed; buried; sunk.
immerse.geminiEx: She immersed herself in her studies to prepare for the final exam.
immerse.geminiTwEx: 為了準備期末考，她全神貫注地投入在學業中。

geminiRaw.ex: She immersed herself in her studies to prepare for the final exam.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 為了準備期末考，她全神貫注地投入在學業中。

immers.geminiEx: She immersed herself in her studies to prepare for the final exam.
immers.geminiTwEx: 為了準備期末考，她全心投入於學業之中。

geminiRaw.ex: She immersed herself in her studies to prepare for the final exam.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 為了準備期末考，她全心投入於學業之中。

expand.tip: To change (something) from a smaller form and/or size to a larger one; to spread out or lay open.
expand.geminiEx: The company plans to expand its operations into international markets.
expand.geminiTwEx: 該公司計劃將業務擴展至國際市場。

geminiRaw.ex: The company plans to expand its operations into international markets.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 該公司計劃將業務擴展至國際市場。

definitions.def: A view, vista or outlook.
The appearance of depth in objects, especially as perceived using binocular vision.
The technique of representing three-dimensional objects on a two-dimensional surface.
Of, in or relating to perspective.
definitions.example: a perspective drawing

definitions.def: Providing visual aid; of or relating to the science of vision; optical.

perspective.geminiEx: From my perspective, this is the best solution to the problem.
perspective.geminiTwEx: 以我的觀點來看，這是解決這個問題最好的方法。

geminiRaw.ex: From my perspective, this is the best solution to the problem.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 以我的觀點來看，這是解決這個問題最好的方法。

perspectif.geminiEx: From my perspective, the plan needs more careful consideration.
perspectif.geminiTwEx: 以我的觀點來看，這項計畫需要更審慎的考量。

geminiRaw.ex: From my perspective, the plan needs more careful consideration.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 以我的觀點來看，這項計畫需要更審慎的考量。

study.tip: (usually academic) To review materials already learned in order to make sure one does not forget them, usually in preparation for an examination.
study.geminiEx: She is studying hard for her final exams.
study.geminiTwEx: 她正在為期末考努力讀書。

geminiRaw.ex: She is studying hard for her final exams.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 她正在為期末考努力讀書。

definitions.def: (usually academic) To review materials already learned in order to make sure one does not forget them, usually in preparation for an examination.
definitions.example: I need to study my biology notes.

definitions.def: (academic) To take a course or courses on a subject.
definitions.example: I study medicine at the university.

definitions.def: To acquire knowledge on a subject with the intention of applying it in practice.
definitions.example: Biologists study living things.

win.geminiEx: She worked hard to win the championship.
win.geminiTwEx: 她努力工作以贏得冠軍。

geminiRaw.ex: She worked hard to win the championship.
geminiRaw.tw_ex: 她努力工作以贏得冠軍。

definitions: [verb] To conquer, defeat.
[verb] To reach some destination or object, despite difficulty or toil (now usually intransitive, with preposition or locative adverb).

utilize.tip: To make use of; to use.

definitions.def: To make use of; to use.
To make useful; to find a practical use for.
To make best use of; to use to its fullest extent, potential, or ability.

utilize.geminiEx: We should utilize the available resources to complete the project on time.
utilize.geminiTwEx: 我們應該利用現有的資源來準時完成這個專案。
